Name: curvature_aware_adaptive_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Adaptive Routing for Training-Free Mixed Precision Quantization
Short Hypothesis: Integrating curvature-aware diagnostics into training-free adaptive routing can lead to optimized mixed-precision quantization strategies that enhance model performance while reducing computational overhead.
Related Work: Current research in mixed-precision quantization, such as EMQ and SNIP, has focused on training-free approaches but often overlooks the potential of curvature diagnostics. This proposal distinguishes itself by explicitly incorporating curvature-aware metrics to guide adaptive routing for mixed-precision quantization, which has not been explored in depth.
Abstract: This research proposes a novel framework for mixed-precision quantization that leverages curvature-aware adaptive routing. By integrating curvature diagnostics into the routing process, we aim to create a training-free approach that optimally allocates precision levels to different model components based on their geometric properties. This method is designed to enhance model performance while minimizing computational costs. We will evaluate the effectiveness of our approach on large language models and vision transformers, comparing it against existing training-free quantization techniques. Our hypothesis is that utilizing curvature information will lead to improved routing decisions, resulting in higher accuracy and efficiency in model inference. This work aims to provide a significant contribution to the field of mixed-precision quantization and adaptive routing, paving the way for more efficient deployment of machine learning models.
Experiments: 1. Develop a curvature-aware routing mechanism that analyzes gradient covariance and Hessian conditioning to inform precision allocation.
2. Implement the proposed routing mechanism in a mixed-precision quantization framework for both LLMs and ViTs.
3. Conduct experiments to compare the performance of the curvature-aware adaptive routing framework against existing training-free mixed-precision methods on benchmark datasets.
4. Evaluate model performance using metrics such as top-1 accuracy, inference time, and energy consumption.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential challenges include the complexity of accurately measuring curvature metrics in high-dimensional spaces and ensuring that the routing mechanisms do not introduce significant overhead. Additionally, the generalizability of the approach across different model architectures needs to be validated.
